texto: Writing is always a restorative process. It's like paddling a kayak. When you're writing, you can't do anything else. You're in the space you're in. So, in that way, it's enormously centering and restorative.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Tabitha King: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Writing is always a restorative process. It's like paddling a kayak. When you're writing, you can…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, writing, process pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de process e you.

frase_original: Writing is always a restorative process. It's like paddling a kayak. When you're writing, you can't do anything else. You're in the space you're in. So, in that way, it's enormously centering and restorative.
